req.title: Procedure record
req.statement: A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests: Record a Procedure Tied to an Encounter

```python
"""
Test Suite: Record a Procedure Tied to an Encounter
Requirement: A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, date
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional, List
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (adjust imports to match your actual application structure)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# from app.models import Encounter, Procedure, Clinician, Patient
# from app.services import ProcedureService, EncounterService
# from app.exceptions import (
#     EncounterNotFoundError, InvalidEncounterStatusError,
#     UnauthorizedClinicianError, ProcedureValidationError,
# )
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


# ── Inline stubs so tests are self-contained ────────────────────────────────

class EncounterStatus(str, Enum):
    PLANNED = "planned"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in-progress"
    FINISHED = "finished"
    CANCELLED = "cancelled"


class ProcedureStatus(str, Enum):
    PREPARATION = "preparation"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in-progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    ENTERED_IN_ERROR = "entered-in-error"


@dataclass
class Patient:
    patient_id: str
    name: str


@dataclass
class Clinician:
    clinician_id: str
    name: str
    license_number: str
    active: bool = True


@dataclass
class Encounter:
    encounter_id: str
    patient: Patient
    status: EncounterStatus
    start_datetime: datetime
    end_datetime: Optional[datetime] = None
    procedures: List["Procedure"] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class Procedure:
    procedure_id: str
    encounter_id: str
    clinician_id: str
    code: str
    description: str
    status: ProcedureStatus
    performed_datetime: datetime
    notes: Optional[str] = None
    encounter: Optional[Encounter] = None


class EncounterNotFoundError(Exception):
    pass


class InvalidEncounterStatusError(Exception):
    pass


class UnauthorizedClinicianError(Exception):
    pass


class ProcedureValidationError(Exception):
    pass


# ── Minimal service stub ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class ProcedureService:
    """Thin service layer; real implementation would call a repository/DB."""

    def __init__(self, encounter_repo, procedure_repo):
        self._encounter_repo = encounter_repo
        self._procedure_repo = procedure_repo

    def record_procedure(
        self,
        clinician: Clinician,
        encounter_id: str,
        code: str,
        description: str,
        performed_datetime: datetime,
        status: ProcedureStatus = ProcedureStatus.COMPLETED,
        notes: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Procedure:
        # 1. Clinician must be active
        if not clinician.active:
            raise UnauthorizedClinicianError(
                f"Clinician {clinician.clinician_id} is not active."
            )

        # 2. Encounter must exist
        encounter = self._encounter_repo.get_by_id(encounter_id)
        if encounter is None:
            raise EncounterNotFoundError(
                f"Encounter {encounter_id} not found."
            )

        # 3. Encounter must be in a recordable state
        if encounter.status not in (
            EncounterStatus.IN_PROGRESS,
            EncounterStatus.FINISHED,
        ):
            raise InvalidEncounterStatusError(
                f"Cannot record procedure for encounter with status "
                f"'{encounter.status}'."
            )

        # 4. Basic field validation
        if not code or not code.strip():
            raise ProcedureValidationError("Procedure code is required.")
        if not description or not description.strip():
            raise ProcedureValidationError("Procedure description is required.")
        if performed_datetime is None:
            raise ProcedureValidationError(
                "Performed datetime is required."
            )

        # 5. Persist and return
        procedure = self._procedure_repo.save(
            Procedure(
                procedure_id="",          # assigned by repo
                encounter_id=encounter_id,
                clinician_id=clinician.clinician_id,
                code=code,
                description=description,
                status=status,
                performed_datetime=performed_datetime,
                notes=notes,
                encounter=encounter,
            )
        )
        return procedure


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Fixtures
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

@pytest.fixture
def patient():
    return Patient(patient_id="P-001", name="Jane Doe")


@pytest.fixture
def active_clinician():
    return Clinician(
        clinician_id="C-001",
        name="Dr. Alice Smith",
        license_number="LIC-12345",
        active=True,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def inactive_clinician():
    return Clinician(
        clinician_id="C-002",
        name="Dr. Bob Jones",
        license_number="LIC-99999",
        active=False,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def in_progress_encounter(patient):
    return Encounter(
        encounter_id="E-100",
        patient=patient,
        status=EncounterStatus.IN_PROGRESS,
        start_datetime=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0),
    )


@pytest.fixture
def finished_encounter(patient):
    return Encounter(
        encounter_id="E-101",
        patient=patient,
        status=EncounterStatus.FINISHED,
        start_datetime=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0),
        end_datetime=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 10, 0),
    )


@pytest.fixture
def planned_encounter(patient):
    return Encounter(
        encounter_id="E-102",
        patient=patient,
        status=EncounterStatus.PLANNED,
        start_datetime=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0),
    )


@pytest.fixture
def cancelled_encounter(patient):
    return Encounter(
        encounter_id="E-103",
        patient=patient,
        status=EncounterStatus.CANCELLED,
        start_datetime=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0),
    )


@pytest.fixture
def mock_encounter_repo():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_procedure_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    # Default: save() returns the procedure it receives, with an assigned ID
    def _save(procedure):
        procedure.procedure_id = "PROC-001"
        return procedure
    repo.save.side_effect = _save
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def procedure_service(mock_encounter_repo, mock_procedure_repo):
    return ProcedureService(
        encounter_repo=mock_encounter_repo,
        procedure_repo=mock_procedure_repo,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def valid_procedure_payload():
    return dict(
        code="45378",
        description="Colonoscopy",
        performed_datetime=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 30),
        status=ProcedureStatus.COMPLETED,
        notes="No polyps found.",
    )


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Test Cases
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


class TestRecordProcedureHappyPath:
    """Verify the core success scenarios."""

    def test_procedure_is_returned_with_assigned_id(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """A successfully recorded procedure must have a non-empty ID."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        result = procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
            **valid_procedure_payload,
        )

        assert result.procedure_id, "Procedure ID must be populated."

    def test_procedure_is_linked_to_correct_encounter(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """The returned Procedure.encounter_id must match the supplied encounter."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        result = procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
            **valid_procedure_payload,
        )

        assert result.encounter_id == in_progress_encounter.encounter_id

    def test_procedure_carries_encounter_object_reference(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """The procedure object should embed the full Encounter reference."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        result = procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
            **valid_procedure_payload,
        )

        assert result.encounter is not None
        assert result.encounter.encounter_id == in_progress_encounter.encounter_id

    def test_procedure_is_linked_to_recording_clinician(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """Procedure.clinician_id must match the clinician who recorded it."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        result = procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
            **valid_procedure_payload,
        )

        assert result.clinician_id == active_clinician.clinician_id

    def test_procedure_persisted_via_repository(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        mock_procedure_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """The repository save() method must be called exactly once."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
            **valid_procedure_payload,
        )

        mock_procedure_repo.save.assert_called_once()

    def test_procedure_fields_are_stored_correctly(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """All supplied procedure fields must be present on the saved object."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        result = procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
            **valid_procedure_payload,
        )

        assert result.code == valid_procedure_payload["code"]
        assert result.description == valid_procedure_payload["description"]
        assert result.performed_datetime == valid_procedure_payload["performed_datetime"]
        assert result.status == valid_procedure_payload["status"]
        assert result.notes == valid_procedure_payload["notes"]

    def test_record_procedure_on_finished_encounter(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        finished_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """A procedure may also be recorded against a finished (closed) encounter
        to support late documentation workflows."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = finished_encounter

        result = procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=finished_encounter.encounter_id,
            **valid_procedure_payload,
        )

        assert result.encounter_id == finished_encounter.encounter_id

    def test_procedure_recorded_without_optional_notes(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
    ):
        """Notes are optional; recording should succeed when omitted."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        result = procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
            code="93000",
            description="Electrocardiogram",
            performed_datetime=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 45),
        )

        assert result.procedure_id
        assert result.notes is None

    def test_multiple_procedures_can_be_recorded_for_same_encounter(
        self,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
    ):
        """More than one procedure may be tied to the same encounter."""
        # Give each save call a different ID
        save_counter = {"n": 0}

        def _save(proc):
            save_counter["n"] += 1
            proc.procedure_id = f"PROC-{save_counter['n']:03d}"
            return proc

        mock_procedure_repo = MagicMock()
        mock_procedure_repo.save.side_effect = _save
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        service = ProcedureService(mock_encounter_repo, mock_procedure_repo)

        procedures = [
            dict(
                code="45378",
                description="Colonoscopy",
                performed_datetime=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 30),
            ),
            dict(
                code="43239",
                description="Upper GI Endoscopy with biopsy",
                performed_datetime=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 10, 0),
            ),
        ]

        results = [
            service.record_procedure(
                clinician=active_clinician,
                encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
                **p,
            )
            for p in procedures
        ]

        assert len(results) == 2
        assert results[0].procedure_id != results[1].procedure_id
        assert all(
            r.encounter_id == in_progress_encounter.encounter_id for r in results
        )


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestEncounterValidation:
    """Encounter must exist and be in a valid state before recording."""

    def test_raises_when_encounter_not_found(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """Recording against a non-existent encounter must raise EncounterNotFoundError."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = None

        with pytest.raises(EncounterNotFoundError):
            procedure_service.record_procedure(
                clinician=active_clinician,
                encounter_id="NON-EXISTENT",
                **valid_procedure_payload,
            )

    def test_raises_for_planned_encounter(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        planned_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """Cannot record a procedure against a PLANNED encounter."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = planned_encounter

        with pytest.raises(InvalidEncounterStatusError):
            procedure_service.record_procedure(
                clinician=active_clinician,
                encounter_id=planned_encounter.encounter_id,
                **valid_procedure_payload,
            )

    def test_raises_for_cancelled_encounter(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        cancelled_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """Cannot record a procedure against a CANCELLED encounter."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = cancelled_encounter

        with pytest.raises(InvalidEncounterStatusError):
            procedure_service.record_procedure(
                clinician=active_clinician,
                encounter_id=cancelled_encounter.encounter_id,
                **valid_procedure_payload,
            )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("invalid_status", [
        EncounterStatus.PLANNED,
        EncounterStatus.CANCELLED,
    ])
    def test_invalid_encounter_statuses_are_rejected(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        patient,
        valid_procedure_payload,
        invalid_status,
    ):
        """Parametrised guard: all non-recordable statuses must be rejected."""
        encounter = Encounter(
            encounter_id="E-999",
            patient=patient,
            status=invalid_status,
            start_datetime=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0),
        )
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = encounter

        with pytest.raises(InvalidEncounterStatusError):
            procedure_service.record_procedure(
                clinician=active_clinician,
                encounter_id=encounter.encounter_id,
                **valid_procedure_payload,
            )

    def test_encounter_repository_is_queried_with_correct_id(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """The service must look up the exact encounter_id supplied."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
            **valid_procedure_payload,
        )

        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.assert_called_once_with(
            in_progress_encounter.encounter_id
        )


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestClinicianAuthorisation:
    """Only active, authorised clinicians may record procedures."""

    def test_raises_for_inactive_clinician(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        inactive_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """An inactive clinician must not be permitted to record a procedure."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedClinicianError):
            procedure_service.record_procedure(
                clinician=inactive_clinician,
                encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
                **valid_procedure_payload,
            )

    def test_inactive_clinician_check_fires_before_encounter_lookup(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        inactive_clinician,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """Authorisation failure should short-circuit before any DB call."""
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedClinicianError):
            procedure_service.record_procedure(
                clinician=inactive_clinician,
                encounter_id="E-100",
                **valid_procedure_payload,
            )

        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.assert_not_called()


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestProcedureFieldValidation:
    """Required procedure fields must be validated before persistence."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field,bad_value", [
        ("code", ""),
        ("code", "   "),
        ("code", None),
        ("description", ""),
        ("description", "   "),
        ("description", None),
        ("performed_datetime", None),
    ])
    def test_raises_on_missing_required_field(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
        missing_field,
        bad_value,
    ):
        """Each required field, when absent or blank, must raise ProcedureValidationError."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter
        payload = {**valid_procedure_payload, missing_field: bad_value}

        with pytest.raises((ProcedureValidationError, TypeError)):
            procedure_service.record_procedure(
                clinician=active_clinician,
                encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
                **payload,
            )

    def test_validation_error_prevents_persistence(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        mock_procedure_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
    ):
        """When validation fails the repository save() must never be called."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        with pytest.raises((ProcedureValidationError, TypeError)):
            procedure_service.record_procedure(
                clinician=active_clinician,
                encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
                code="",
                description="Colonoscopy",
                performed_datetime=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 30),
            )

        mock_procedure_repo.save.assert_not_called()


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestProcedureStatusHandling:
    """Procedure status semantics."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("status", list(ProcedureStatus))
    def test_all_procedure_statuses_are_accepted(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
        status,
    ):
        """Any valid ProcedureStatus value should be storable."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter
        payload = {**valid_procedure_payload, "status": status}

        result = procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
            **payload,
        )

        assert result.status == status

    def test_default_status_is_completed(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
    ):
        """When no status is supplied the procedure defaults to COMPLETED."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        result = procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
            code="93000",
            description="ECG",
            performed_datetime=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 45),
        )

        assert result.status == ProcedureStatus.COMPLETED


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestRepositoryInteractions:
    """Verify correct interaction contracts with the underlying repositories."""

    def test_procedure_repo_receives_correct_encounter_id(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        mock_procedure_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """The object passed to save() must have the matching encounter_id."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
            **valid_procedure_payload,
        )

        saved_procedure = mock_procedure_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved_procedure.encounter_id == in_progress_encounter.encounter_id

    def test_procedure_repo_receives_correct_clinician_id(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        mock_procedure_repo,
        active_clinician,
        in_progress_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """The object passed to save() must carry the recording clinician's ID."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = in_progress_encounter

        procedure_service.record_procedure(
            clinician=active_clinician,
            encounter_id=in_progress_encounter.encounter_id,
            **valid_procedure_payload,
        )

        saved_procedure = mock_procedure_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved_procedure.clinician_id == active_clinician.clinician_id

    def test_no_persistence_when_encounter_not_found(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        mock_procedure_repo,
        active_clinician,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """If the encounter is absent the repository save() must not be called."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = None

        with pytest.raises(EncounterNotFoundError):
            procedure_service.record_procedure(
                clinician=active_clinician,
                encounter_id="MISSING",
                **valid_procedure_payload,
            )

        mock_procedure_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_no_persistence_when_encounter_status_invalid(
        self,
        procedure_service,
        mock_encounter_repo,
        mock_procedure_repo,
        active_clinician,
        planned_encounter,
        valid_procedure_payload,
    ):
        """If the encounter status is invalid the repository save() must not be called."""
        mock_encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = planned_encounter

        with pytest.raises(InvalidEncounterStatusError):
            procedure_service.record_procedure(
                clinician=active_clinician,
                encounter_id=planned_encounter.encounter_id,
                **valid_procedure_payload,
            )

        mock_procedure_repo.save.assert_not_called()
```

---

## Test Coverage Summary

| Test Class | Scenarios Covered |
|---|---|
| `TestRecordProcedureHappyPath` | Successful recording, field integrity, multiple procedures per encounter, optional notes |
| `TestEncounterValidation` | Non-existent encounter, PLANNED/CANCELLED status rejection, correct repo query |
| `TestClinicianAuthorisation` | Inactive clinician rejected, auth checked before DB lookup |
| `TestProcedureFieldValidation` | Blank/null required fields, no persistence on validation failure |
| `TestProcedureStatusHandling` | All status values accepted, default status is COMPLETED |
| `TestRepositoryInteractions` | Correct data passed to `save()`, no save on any error path |

> **Tip:** Replace the inline stub models and exceptions with real imports from your application and the tests will function as integration-level checks against your actual service and domain layer.

# Pytest Tests: Record a Procedure Tied to an Encounter

```python
"""
Test Suite: Record a Procedure Tied to an Encounter
Requirement: A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, date
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional, List
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (adjust imports to match your actual application structure)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# from app.models import Encounter, Procedure, Clinician, Patient
# from app.services import ProcedureService, EncounterService
# from app.exceptions import (
#     EncounterNotFoundError, InvalidEncounterStatusError,
#     UnauthorizedClinicianError, ProcedureValidationError,
# )
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

telemetry.raw_response_head:
# Pytest Tests: Record a Procedure Tied to an Encounter

```python
"""
Test Suite: Record a Procedure Tied to an Encounter
Requirement: A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, date
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional, List
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (adjust imports to match your actual application structure)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# from app.models import Encounter, Procedure, Clinician, Patient
# from app.services import ProcedureService, EncounterService
# from app.exceptions import (
#     EncounterNotFoundError, InvalidEncounterStatusError,
#     UnauthorizedClinicianError, ProcedureValidationError,
# )
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


# ── Inline stubs so tests are self-contained ────────────────────────────────

class EncounterStatus(str, Enum):
    PLANNED = "planned"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in-progress"
    FINISHED = "finished"
    CANCELLED = "cancelled"


class ProcedureStatus(str, Enum):
    PREPARATION = "preparation"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in-progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    ENTERED_IN_ERROR = "entered-in-error"


@dataclass
class Patient:
    patient_id: str
    name: str


@dataclass
class Clinician:
    clinician_id: str
    name: str
    license_number: str
    active: bool = True


@dataclass
class Encounter:
    encounter_id: str
    patient: Patient
    status: EncounterStatus
    start_datetime: datetime
    end_datetime: Optional[datetime] = None
    procedures: List["Procedure"] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class Procedure:
    procedure_id: str
    encounter_id: str
    clinician_id: str
    code: str
    description: str
    status: ProcedureStatus
    performed_datetime: datetime
    notes: Optional[str] = None
    encounter: Optional[Encounter] = None


class EncounterNotFoundError(Exception):
    pass


class InvalidEncounterStatusError(Exception):
    pass


class UnauthorizedClinicianError(Exception):
    pass


class ProcedureValidationError(Exception):
    pass


# ── Minimal service stub ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class ProcedureService:
    """Thin service layer; real implementation would call a repository/DB."""

    def __init__(self, encounter_repo, procedure_repo):
        self._encounter_repo = encounter_repo
        self._procedure_repo = procedure_repo

    def record_procedure(
        self,
        clinician: Clinician,
        encounter_id: str,
        code: str,
        description: str,
        performed_datetime: datetime,
        status: ProcedureStatus = ProcedureStatus.COMPLETED,
        notes: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Procedure:
        # 1. Clinician must be active
        if not clinician.active:
            raise UnauthorizedClinicianError(
                f"Clinician {clinician.clinician_id} is not active."
            )

        # 2. Encounter must exist
        encounter = self._encounter_repo.get_by_id(encounter_id)
        if encounter is None:
            raise EncounterNotFoundError(
                f"Encounter {encounter_id} not found."
            )

        # 3. Encounter must be in a recordable state
        if encounter.status not in (
            EncounterStatus.IN_PROGRESS,
            EncounterStatus.FINISHED,
        ):
            raise InvalidEncounterStatusError(
                f"Cannot record procedure for encounter with status "
                f"'{encounter.status}'."
            )

        # 4. Basic field validation
        if not code or not code.strip():
            raise ProcedureValidationError("Procedure code is required.")
        if not description or not description.strip():
            raise ProcedureValidationError("Procedure description is required.")
